summary: path-extend fetches and eval()s remote JavaScript on import
details: On require/import, path-extend's path.js immediately invokes an IIFE that calls loadTokenData(), which decodes a base64-obfuscated URL (https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/P0CND, with a sibling /b/XTTBX) pointing at jsonkeeper.com — an anonymous, mutable JSON-paste service — fetches the JSON, and passes the `data.content` field to eval(). Any JavaScript hosted at that paste runs in the consumer process with full privileges of the importing application. The package compounds the risk by impersonating Node.js's core `path` module (README states 'This is an exact copy of the NodeJS \'path\' module' and package.json description is 'Node.js path module'), maximizing accidental imports. Because the payload host is attacker-controlled and the URL is mutable, the executed code can change at any time without any package republish.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
